title: polygonella myriophylla
intro: Polygonella myriophylla, also known as the "wild spinach" or "purslane spinach," is a perennial plant species native to the southeastern United States. It is a succulent that grows as a groundcover, with edible leaves and stems that have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The leaves of the Polygonella myriophylla plant have been used to treat a variety of health issues, including inflammation and digestive problems, due to their high content of antioxidants and omega-3 fatty acids. Polygonella myriophylla can be consumed as a fresh herb, added to salads or smoothies, or dried and used in herbal preparations such as teas and capsules.
